$comment: Machine index for the RAG library. Skeleton/template state: one example book + the reference-doc set. Fill books[], topics, and counts during onboarding (see LIBRARY_SEED.md). Keep valid JSON; counts must match reality; coverage_gaps must be honest.

section_index.purpose: Every heading's id/title/line. Resolve and verify any 'Book NN §X' without opening the book. Regenerate with tools/_gen_sections.py.

primary_assumptions.note: Unless the project says otherwise, the library assumes these. Architectural patterns transfer across stacks; specific APIs do not.

intended_uses: Study path: a human reads the books to learn the domain.
RAG reference: an agent consults the library while building, citing exact sections.

books.title: The Book Template (How To Write A Book In This Library)
books.summary: The book template: how to write a new book in this library — the section-id grammar (## N. / ### N.M / ## N{Letter}.), the standard arc (TL;DR to foundations to architecture to implementation to frontier to agent-integration to exercises), and citation discipline. Copy it to author a real book.

reference_docs.summary: Back-of-book topical index: find where any topic is covered, by Book NN and section.
Alphabetized term definitions, each pinned to the book(s) that cover it.
Cataloged failure modes — symptom, diagnosis, fix, and the books — the common mistakes to avoid.
Recurring architectural decisions as flowcharts with trade-offs; numbered D1, D2, and mirrored by the project's decision log.
Symptom to diagnosis lookup keyed on plain vocabulary (X is slow / wrong / flaky / broken) routing to anti-patterns and sections.
Named recurring techniques — the shared vocabulary of the domain.
Recipe-style multi-book task guides for composite operations.
Milestone-driven path from zero to a shipped result, with exit criteria; the build order the ROADMAP mirrors.

skills.description: Resume a session: read Status + ROADMAP + open issues, pick up the resume point.
Checkpoint before context runs out: rewrite Status, update docs, file followups, push.
Drive LIBRARY_SEED.md to author/extend the textbook/RAG library and keep the audits green.
Inspect the repo and fill PROJECT_CONVENTIONS.md.
Library-grounded implementation plan; the planning front-end.
Conformance audit of a design/diff vs the library, cited Must-fix/Consider.
Multi-lens independent review before merge; fans out reviewers to falsify claims.
Run the full verification gate with per-gate evidence.
Full build + test cycle using PROJECT_CONVENTIONS commands.
Add unit/integration/property/golden tests.
Capture and analyze a profile for a named subsystem.
Profile-driven optimization of a hot path.
Agentic LLM content-generation pipeline with validation.
Add a telemetry event with schema + sampling.
Add a typed, validated, permission-filtered MCP tool.
Round-trip snapshot/restore test for any state-owning subsystem.
CI gate: initializes/ticks/shuts down with no window/device/human.
Ensure deferred work lives in the tracker, not only in chat.
Survey a frontier topic online into a sourced + tiered research note (real fetched links only).
Pre-registered, reproducible experiment with comparable, plottable results (EXP-NN).
Markdown-first paper artifact (RR-NN) with real-link references and regenerable figures.

coverage_gaps.description: Topics the library does NOT cover or covers only partially. Check here FIRST when asked about something hard to locate, and say so honestly rather than fabricate. Fill this in as the library grows.

gaps.nearest: none yet — this is a freshly templated library; only the book template (book_00) exists
